'''James Greenwood''' (born 7 June 1991) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} and {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], spending time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales]] in League 1, the [[London Broncos]], [[Salford Red Devils]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League and [[Workington Town]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Greenwood also played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League.

==Background==
Greenwood was born in [[Oldham]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

===Personal life===
He is the brother of fellow [[rugby league]] footballer, [[Joe Greenwood]].{{citation needed|date=June 2025}}

==Early career==
Greenwood started his amateur playing career at the Saddleworth Rangers and he is a product of the [[Wigan Warriors]]' Academy System.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

==Senior career==
[[File:Jimmy Greenwood.jpg|thumb|right|Greenwood playing for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in 2016]]

===South Wales Scorpions===
====2012====
In 2012, he played for [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]], Greenwood only appeared sporadically for the club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====2013====
He returned to the [[South Wales Ironmen|Scorpions]] in the 2013 [[rugby league]] season on a loan basis.<ref name="loverugbyleague">{{cite web|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/players/James-Greenwood|title=James Greenwood Player Stats|publisher=loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=2014-05-28}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
====2013====
In 2013, Greenwood was transferred to the [[Wigan Warriors]], where he was then subsequently loaned back to his former club the [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] shortly after.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===London Broncos===
====2014====
In 2014, from his parent-club the [[Wigan Warriors]] he was loaned out again to the then [[Super League]] outfit the [[London Broncos]], where he regularly played for them as a {{rlp|pr}}.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Workington Town===
====2015====
He spent time on loan at [[Workington Town]] in 2015, as part of a dual-registration agreement.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Salford Red Devils===
====2015====
In 2015, Greenwood was loaned to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] for a short period of time.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Hull Kingston Rovers===

====2015====
Greenwood was sent out once again on loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2015, this time to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], where he impressed enough to turn his loan-deal from the [[Wigan Warriors]] into a full-time two-year contract.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====2016 & 2017====
Greenwood suffered relegation from the [[Super League]] with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2016 season, due to losing the [[Million Pound Game]] at the hands of [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].

12-months later however, Greenwood was part of the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://saddind.co.uk/success-for-greenwood-brothers-on-opposite-sides-of-the-world/|title=Success for Greenwood brothers on opposite sides of the world|website=saddind.co.uk|date=7 October 2017 }}</ref>

====2018====
Greenwood made 23 appearances for Hull KR in all competitions as the club finished 10th on the table and avoided relegation.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nothingbutleague.com/2018/11/19/2018-season-review-hull-kingston-rovers/|title=Hull Kingston Rovers 2018 season review|website=nothingbutleague.com|date=19 November 2018 }}</ref>

====2019====
On 14 March, Greenwood was taken from the field during round 5 of the 2019 Super League season against [[Wakefield Trinity]].  He was later ruled out for the entire year after scans revealed he ruptured his anterior cruciate knee ligament.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-star-james-greenwood-ruled-out-for-the-season/|title=Hull KR star James Greenwood ruled out for the season|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=14 March 2019 }}</ref>

===Salford Red Devils===
====2020====
In 2020, Greenwood joined Salford and later played for the club in their [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat against Leeds.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
On 29 November 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=29 Nov 2023|title=Former Salford Red Devils, Hull KR forward makes Championship move for 2024: 'It's clear to see the hunger he has for the game'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-salford-red-devils-hull-kr-forward-james-greenwood-makes-championship-barrow-raiders-move-for-2024}}</ref>

On 1 November 2025 it was reported that he had left [[Barrow Raiders]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=1 Nov 2025|accessdate=2 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Super League Grand Final winner among quartet of departures from Championship club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/aaron-smith-among-four-departures-from-barrow}}</ref>
